methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $43M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: REGENT Craft
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series A), post-money $270M

data.narrative:
FleetZero is a Series A transportation company founded by Steven Henderson and Mike Carter that has raised $43M to date. The broader transportation sector includes 8 private comparable companies—notably REGENT Craft, Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, and Harbinger—though no directly comparable public peers exist. The lack of same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the transportation universe limits peer-median approaches.

Given data constraints, stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable valuation lens. Using the $43M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression yields $86M. This method rests on the assumption that capital raised tracks pre-money valuation at standard Series A economics.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $73M (low) to $99M (high), with a median point of $86M. The narrow range reflects reliance on a single quantifiable method rather than consensus across multiple approaches. Key uncertainties include the absence of narrow-set peer pricing data, which would otherwise anchor relative valuation, and the dependency on stage-progression step-up factors that may not reflect FleetZero's specific unit economics or capital efficiency. Revenue, burn rate, and customer traction data would materially sharpen this analysis.
